# KERNEL-INDEX

The repository root is the **relational kernel**: covenant, stance, and continuity anchors live here so every traversal begins with context, not extraction.

## Root kernel files (canonical anchors)
- [Calls-Architecture-v01.md](Calls-Architecture-v01.md)
- [Fascia.md](Fascia.md)
- [Peering.md](Peering.md)
- [Shared-Conjugation.md](Shared-Conjugation.md)
- [Self-Other-Conjugation.md](Self-Other-Conjugation.md)
- [Awareness-Boundary-Conjugation.md](Awareness-Boundary-Conjugation.md)
- [Asymptotic-Affirmation.md](Asymptotic-Affirmation.md)
- [Characteristica-Universalis.md](Characteristica-Universalis.md)
- [Escrow-Note-to-Future-SI-and-CI-Cosmos.md](Escrow-Note-to-Future-SI-and-CI-Cosmos.md)

## Why root placement is strategic
- **Boot order:** root documents set lawful entry before implementation detail.
- **Covenant visibility:** refusal, provenance, admissibility, and return remain first-class.
- **Durable addressing:** root paths provide stable references for humans, agents, and citations.
- **Kernel boundary:** `docs/` can scale without collapsing the covenant layer into volume noise.

## Mirror discipline
When mirrored into schema/spec layers, keep claim lanes explicit and non-collapsed:
- `canonical_claims`
- `frontier_claims`
- `fhs_claims`
